meta.title: FAQ & Contact | Padel2Go – Help and Support
meta.description: Find answers to common questions about Padel2Go or contact us directly. We support players, clubs, and partners.

hero.title: Questions? We have
hero.description: Player, club, or partner — you'll find the important info here. And if you can't: just write to us!

contact.heading: Get in
contact.intro: Have a question or want to learn more? Write to us — we reply fast!

form.heading: Send us a message
form.organizationPlaceholder: e.g. TC Bamberg, Red Bull, Süddeutsche Zeitung

vereine.heading: For Clubs
vereine.body: Interested in padel courts for your club? We offer a free initial consultation including site check and potential analysis.

partner.heading: For Partners
partner.body: Want to become a partner? Request our partner deck with all the info on categories, reach, and successful cases.

faq.heading: Frequently asked
faq.intro: Sorted by audience: players, clubs, and partners.

questions.a: No, no membership required! Padel2Go is open to everyone. You just download the app, create a free account, and can book any Padel2Go location right away — whether you're a club member or not.

questions.q: How do I book a court?
questions.a: Open the Padel2Go app, pick your preferred location, and see all available times. Your court is booked in a few taps. You'll get an access code to enter the court on time.

questions.q: What does an hour of padel cost?
questions.a: Prices vary by location and time of day, but typically range from €30–50 per hour for the entire court (4 players). That's just €7.50–12.50 per person — cheaper than many other sports!

questions.q: Do I need my own equipment?
questions.a: No! Every location has vending machines with rackets and balls to rent or buy. All you need are sports shoes (ideally tennis shoes or running shoes with grip).
Instant Match automatically finds players at your skill level when you're short of a full court. Perfect if you come alone or as a pair. The app matches you based on skill level and playing time.

questions.q: How does the rewards system work?
questions.a: You earn points with every booking, every match, and every activity. Redeem them for discounts, free hours, partner products, and exclusive experiences. The more you play, the more you benefit!

questions.q: What does a court cost the club?
questions.a: Nothing at all! Padel2Go covers all costs for courts, installation, maintenance, insurance, and marketing. Your club provides the space and earns an attractive revenue share — completely risk-free.

Tennis clay courts are ideal (we install mobile courts that can be removed in winter), but parking lots, open spaces, lawns, or other flat areas from ~200 m² (20×10 m) also work. We check your space for suitability free of charge.
From go-ahead to a court ready to play it typically takes 4–8 weeks. The actual installation only takes 2–3 days. We handle all approvals and the full logistics.

questions.q: How does the revenue share work?
questions.a: You receive a percentage of all booking revenue at your location — with no risk and no upfront investment. We discuss the exact terms individually, depending on location, space, and expected utilization.
Our mobile courts are designed so they can be dismantled if needed and reinstalled in spring. Alternatively, air-supported domes or other roofing can enable year-round operation.
Padel2Go takes care of all maintenance: regular checks, repairs, court cleaning, and refilling the vending machines. Zero effort for you — we handle it.

questions.q: How do I become a partner?
questions.a: Contact us via the form below with the subject 'Partnership inquiry'. We'll send you our partner deck with all the info on categories, reach, and cases. Then we'll set up a call to find the right partnership fit.

We distinguish between product partners (e.g. sportswear, drinks), service partners (e.g. physiotherapy, coaching), media partners, and main sponsors. Each category comes with tailored activation options.

We reach a growing community of active padel players in the DACH region. Our users are above-average in sports affinity and digital engagement and rank among the early adopters of padel — a high-value audience for brands.

questions.q: What does a partnership look like in practice?
questions.a: It depends on your goals: sampling at events, court branding, in-app integration, co-branded rewards, tournament sponsorship, or exclusive experiences. We design the right package together.
